steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student concludes that Glory and Boss "behaved similarly." Supporting data must show both Glory and Boss doing the same thing — either both moving toward or both moving away from the sound. Any choice that introduces Bitey Boy or shows Glory and Boss doing opposite things fails.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Boss moved toward the sound, while Bitey Boy moved away from it.: Compares Boss to Bitey Boy, not to Glory. This tells us nothing about whether Glory and Boss behaved similarly. ✗
  • Choice B — Bitey Boy and Glory both moved toward the sound.: Compares Bitey Boy to Glory, leaving Boss out entirely. Does not address whether Glory and Boss matched each other. ✗
  • Choice C — Glory and Boss both moved away from the sound.: Directly compares the two snakes named in the conclusion and shows identical behavior. This is exactly what "behaved similarly" requires. ✓
  • Choice D — Boss moved toward the sound, while Glory moved away from it.: Glory and Boss moved in opposite directions — this directly contradicts the conclusion that they behaved similarly. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
